name: Free fall with linear (Stokes) air drag
proposal: Extend the free-fall-uniform-gravity card by adding a viscous drag force F_drag = -b v opposing the velocity. The equation of motion becomes m dv/dt = -m g - b v (taking up positive). At long times the velocity asymptotes to a terminal value v_t = -m g / b; in the b -> 0 limit the equation must reduce to standard free fall.

conservationLaws.statement: Mechanical energy is NOT conserved — drag dissipates kinetic energy as heat. Instead: dE/dt = -b v^2 <= 0 (monotone non-increasing), and delta KE = W_gravity + W_drag must hold instantaneously.

references: Stokes (1851) — On the Effect of the Internal Friction of Fluids on the Motion of Pendulums
rationale: Linear drag is the textbook small-Reynolds correction to free fall — first-order term in the velocity expansion of any drag law. Hypothesis recorded so the cross-check engine verifies the b -> 0 limit collapses to the canonical free-fall card.
